panels.description: Request success rate. Above 99.5% is healthy (green), below 98% is critical (red). Only requests with finished_reason='stop' are counted as successful.
panels.title: Success Rate

panels.description: Current running + waiting request count. A sharp increase signals a traffic spike.
panels.title: Active Requests

panels.description: Scheduler efficiency. Running/(Running+Waiting). 100% means all requests are processed immediately. Below 70% indicates overload.
panels.title: System Efficiency

panels.description: P99 time to first token (TTFT). Key metric for perceived initial response speed. Ideally under 1 second.
panels.title: TTFT P99

panels.description: [Critical] Preemption rate. Running requests are interrupted due to KV cache exhaustion. If above 0, investigate immediately. Consider increasing GPU memory or reducing batch size.
panels.title: Preemption Rate

panels.description: End-to-end request latency from receipt to completion. P50 = half of requests, P99 = 99% of requests complete within this time. Investigate bottlenecks if P99 spikes.
panels.title: E2E Request Latency

panels.description: Prefill (input processing) vs Decode (output generation) latency breakdown. High prefill = long input prompts are the bottleneck. High decode = long output generation is the bottleneck.
panels.title: Inference Stage Breakdown

panels.description: TTFT (time to first token) vs TPOT (time per output token). High TTFT = prefill bottleneck. High TPOT = decode bottleneck.
panels.title: TTFT vs TPOT

panels.description: Tokens processed per second. Input (prompt) tokens and output (generation) tokens. Higher throughput = better system efficiency.
panels.title: Token Throughput

panels.description: Input to output token ratio. High = long input + short output (RAG). Low = short input + long output (generation/summarization).
panels.title: Token I/O Ratio

panels.description: Computation saved by prefix cache. Higher = more prefill computation skipped, saving GPU resources. Formula: (1 - actual_compute/total_input) * 100
panels.title: Prefix Cache Savings

panels.description: Distribution of input (prompt) token counts per request. Darker color = more requests at that length. If concentrated in a specific range, consider optimizing for that length.
panels.title: Request Length Heatmap (Prompt)

panels.description: Time requests spend waiting in the queue. Increases when GPU is busy. If consistently high, consider scaling out.
panels.title: Request Queue Time

panels.description: GPU KV cache utilization. Memory region storing intermediate computation results. Above 80% = caution. Above 95% = risk of preemption.
panels.title: GPU KV Cache Usage

panels.description: Prefix cache hit rate. Rate of reusing identical system prompts or few-shot examples. Above 70% is efficient. Key metric in RAG environments.
panels.title: Prefix Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Request count by scheduler state. Running = inferencing on GPU, Waiting = in queue, Swapped = swapped to memory. Sustained increase in Waiting indicates overload.
panels.title: Scheduler State

panels.description: Python GC count and process memory usage. Frequent GC or steadily increasing RSS may indicate a memory leak.
panels.title: Python GC & Memory

panels.description: Request completion reason distribution. stop = normal completion (EOS), length = max length reached, abort = interrupted. High abort rate indicates a problem.
panels.title: Finish Reason Distribution

panels.description: Total request throughput vs successful requests. A gap indicates failed requests. They should consistently match.
panels.title: Throughput vs Success
